title: RESIDUAL HARDENING (primary fix LANDED c2258b72): a context-cancel of an already-finished phase's bridge session is laundered into ExitArtifactTimeout, making a canceled-after-harvest session LOOK like an artifact timeout — clean the telemetry at the driver source so a benign teardown never masquerades as a phase timeout
summary: PRIMARY DEFECT FIXED 2026-07-14 (operator, commit c2258b72, ship --class manual): cycles 824/825 (2 of 4 width-2 storm failures) discarded genuinely-PASS audited cycles because the reconcile-on-timeout block (runner.go, `if errors.Is(bridgeErr, core.ErrArtifactTimeout)`) called verifyFn EXACTLY ONCE, at the instant a next-phase (retro) context-cancel was laundered into ExitArtifactTimeout while the auditor's PASS deliverable was still SETTLING to disk — the single read saw a half-written file and the mandatory phase synthesized VerdictFAIL. FIX: `verifyReconcileDeliverable` re-verifies a bounded number of times (reconcileSettleRetries=3 x reconcileSettleInterval=200ms, injectable SleepFn seam) so a settled deliverable is caught; reconciliation still only UPGRADES a timeout (never-settling stays FAIL, bounded, no spin). This completes the reconcile block's OWN documented intent ('trust a deliverable written just as the bridge gave up on the wait window') and is proven by two RED->GREEN tests driving the LIVE r.Run() path (TestRun_Timeout_DeliverableSettlesOnRetry_ReconcilesToPass = the deliverable-PASS-beats-session-lifecycle-FAIL case; TestRun_Timeout_DeliverableNeverSettles_StillFailsBounded = the bound), each failing if the call site is removed (WIRING PROOF satisfied — no cargo-cult). The settle-retry SUBSUMES the teardown-ordering race for the observed failure (any settle within 600ms reconciles). RESIDUAL (this item, demoted 0.95->0.5): two OPTIONAL hardenings that clean the ROOT rather than the reconcile symptom — (A, cleaner telemetry, the reason this item survives) driver_tmux_repl.go's wait loop breaks on ctx.Err() WITHOUT setting completed=true and returns ExitArtifactTimeout: a context-cancel of an already-finished/harvested session should return a distinct benign 'session-canceled' outcome, NOT masquerade as an artifact timeout, so telemetry (audit-usage.json, dispatch classifier) stops mislabeling clean teardowns as timeouts; (B, lower value now) harvest-before-cancel ordering so the next phase does not cancel the prior phase's session until its deliverable verdict is harvested. Neither is a false-FAIL risk anymore (the settle-retry closes that); they are correctness-of-telemetry + defense-in-depth. NOT a blocker for re-enabling width-2.
acceptance: RED: TestSessionCancelAfterHarvest_ReturnsBenignNotTimeout — a context-cancel landing AFTER the deliverable is written/harvested returns a distinct benign session-canceled outcome (NOT ExitArtifactTimeout), so downstream telemetry records cleanup, not a timeout
RED (optional B): TestAuditSession_HarvestedBeforeNextPhaseStarts — starting the next phase does not context-cancel the prior phase's session until its deliverable verdict is harvested
WIRING PROOF: the benign-cancel path is reached from the LIVE bridge wait-loop (driver_tmux_repl.go), provable by an integration test that fails if the distinction is removed
no regression: internal/bridge + internal/phases/runner go test -race PASS; the settle-retry (c2258b72) remains the safety net if this driver-level change regresses
width-2 soak: audit-usage.json for a cleanly-canceled harvested session shows the deliverable verdict, not a laundered timeout FAIL
source: PRIMARY FIXED c2258b72 (settle-retry, operator 2026-07-14); residual = driver-level cancel-vs-timeout telemetry distinction. Original: cycles 824+825 retrospective [D1] (audit-usage.json session-lifecycle FAIL vs audit-report.md PASS 0.95).

landed.what: verifyReconcileDeliverable bounded settle-retry + SleepFn seam in go/internal/phases/runner/runner.go; 2 new RED->GREEN tests + 3 timeout-path tests given no-op SleepFn
landed.verify: go test -race ./internal/phases/runner/... PASS; go vet + golangci-lint clean; ship --class manual attested
